name: Tea Smoked Duck
recipeIngredient: 4 lb To 5 lb. duck (washed and dried)
1 cup Strong black tea leaves (such as Darjeeling)
recipeInstructions:
The Chinese method of home smoking is surprisingly easy and eliminates duck's fattiness while preserving its flavor. Serve tea-smoked duck as an appetizer at room temperature with toothpicks, or use the meat in stir-fries.
1. Remove any fat from the cavity of the duck and prick the bird all over.
2. Bring an inch or two of water and 6 tbsp soy sauce to a boil in a wok with a tight-fitting lid.
3. Add star anise and cinnamon. Place the duck on a rack in the wok, cover, and steam over simmering water just until the juices run clear, about 40 minutes.
4. Remove the duck from the wok, pouring out any juice from the cavity, and set the bird aside. Discard liquid, rinse the wok, and dry it with paper towels.
5. Line the wok with heavy aluminum foil, allowing the foil to hang about 2 inches over the edge. Place rice, tea, and sugar in the bottom of the wok and mix well.
6. Place a rack about 1 inch above the rice mixture (or place four chopsticks across the wok so they crisscross).
7. Place the duck on the rack, breast-side up. Cover with foil, allowing at least 1 inch between the duck and foil and letting the foil cover hang about 2 inches over the edge.
8. Cover with the lid. Turn heat to high (turn on exhaust fan) and cook until smoke begins to emerge from the wok, 3 to 5 minutes.
9. Crimp the top and bottom edges of the overhanging foil together and smoke for 10 minutes. Reduce heat to medium and smoke for 20 minutes longer.
10. Turn off heat and, leaving foil sealed, let sit for 15 minutes.
11. Unwrap duck and discard tea mixture and aluminum foil. The duck can be smoked up to 2 days ahead and stored, well covered, in the refrigerator. Bring to room temperature before serving.
12. Remove and discard skin. Carve breast meat into thin slices. Cut legs from the carcass (discard carcass) and separate thigh from drumstick at the joint. Mix remaining 6 tbsp soy sauce and sesame oil and brush over the meat. Makes about 1/2 lb cooked meat.
Serves 4 as an appetizer.
128 CALORIES PER SERVING: 14 G PROTEIN, 7 G FAT, 1 G CARBOHYDRATE; 381 MG SODIUM; 51 MG CHOLESTEROL.
